commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is indicated; maintainers need to choose MXC availability direction, stack order, and acceptable runtime/security-boundary risk before merge.; Cleared: No concrete remaining security or supply-chain defect was found in the current diff, but the sandbox-boundary and native SDK changes remain maintainer-visible merge risks. (sha=ca995626a2b1f02d1a7bbc9b1e3e9754f0ba99c0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR bumps `@microsoft/mxc-sdk` to 0.7.0, adds explicit Windows build 26200.8728 MXC support, adjusts `system.run` sandbox fallback/shell/env behavior, adds a strict fallback-blocking setting, updates Sandbox UI diagnostics, and expands MXC/system.run tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a feature/validation PR, not a reported current-main failure. The PR body and comments provide Windows 26200.8728 runtime/log proof for the intended MXC path, but I did not run Windows MXC locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; maintainers need to choose MXC availability direction, stack order, and acceptable runtime/security-boundary risk before merge.

target.title: Validate MXC SDK 0.7.0 on Windows build 26200.8728
target.body:
Tracking issue: #784
Depends on: #785

Contributor-fork note: this branch is stacked on the PR1 commit, but the PR targets `main` because the PR1 base branch exists in the contributor fork, not in the upstream repository. After PR1 is merged or maintainers provide a preferred upstream base branch, this PR can be rebased/retargeted so the review diff only contains the MXC changes.

## Summary

This PR updates the Windows MXC dependency and availability gate for explicit build/UBR validation:

- Update `@microsoft/mxc-sdk` from `0.6.1` to `0.7.0`.
- Replace the single hardcoded supported Windows build with an explicit temporary processcontainer support table.
- Add processcontainer support for Windows build `26200.8728+`.
- Preserve processcontainer support for Windows build `26300.8289+`.
- Keep `isolation_session` separately gated to `26300.8553+`, matching SDK `0.7.0` documentation/source.
- Emit MXC config schema `0.7.0-alpha` for SDK `0.7.0`.
- Keep AppContainer-DACL fallback compatible by avoiding protected PATH-directory grants, omitting nonexistent denied-path roots, and granting only backend-safe PATH directories.
- Bootstrap `TEMP`, `TMP`, `TMPDIR`, and `PATH` inside the launched shell because non-empty MXC `process.env` is not usable on this backend.
- Preserve compatibility by default: with `SystemRunSandboxEnabled=true`, `system.run` uses MXC when available and falls back to the host runner when MXC is unavailable or disappears at runtime.
- Add `SystemRunBlockHostFallbackWhenMxcUnavailable=false` as an explicit opt-in strict/fail-closed policy for operators who want sandbox-unavailable commands blocked instead of host fallback.
- Reject custom `system.run` environment variables before any availability fallback while sandboxing remains enabled, so env-bearing MXC-mode requests do not silently downgrade to host execution.
- Keep MXC omitted-shell execution on `cmd` for this processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL tier; normal uncontained host fallback still resolves through the host runner.
- Resolve explicit `shell="pwsh"` to an absolute `pwsh.exe` path before launching with an empty MXC process environment.
- Update Sandbox page status/InfoBar text so unavailable MXC clearly distinguishes host fallback, strict blocked mode, and sandbox-off host execution.
- Add/extend unit coverage for SDK `0.7.0`, build/UBR gates, config generation, fallback compatibility, strict unavailable behavior, settings persistence, and shell resolution.

This implements ClawSweeper maintainer option 2 from the earlier review: **Preserve Compatibility By Default**. This PR no longer claims fail-closed as the default upgrade behavior; strict fail-closed is an explicit setting.

## Why

The local machine is Windows Release Preview 25H2 build `26200.8728`. The MXC SDK probe can report usable processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL support locally, but OpenClaw's current availability gate rejects the machine because it only allows build `26300`.

This PR keeps the gate explicit and narrow. It does not add a broad `>= 26200` rule and does not enable `isolation_session` on `26200`.

## Validation

Local validation on Windows Release Preview 25H2 build `26200.8728`:

- `./build.ps1`: passed in the earlier PR2 validation pass after the `isolation_session` gate fix.
- Focused `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` filter `MxcAvailabilityTests`: passed, `19` total, `0` failed in the earlier PR2 validation pass.
- Focused `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` filter `MxcAvailabilityTests|MxcConfigBuilderTests|MxcPolicyBuilderTests|MxcCommandRunnerTests|DirectAppContainerExecutorTests`: passed, `90` total, `0` failed in the earlier PR2 validation pass.
- Focused secure-contract filter `MxcCommandRunnerTests|MxcCommandRunnerIntegrationTests|MxcConfigBuilderTests|MxcPolicyBuilderTests|LocalCommandRunnerTests|SystemRunTests|ExecApprovalPolicyTests`: passed, `148` passed, `4` skipped, `0` failed in the earlier PR2 validation pass.
- Final current pass for commit `ddba6f8`: `git diff --cached --check` passed before commit.
- Final current pass for commit `ddba6f8`: `dotnet test .\tests\OpenClaw.Shared.Tests\OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~MxcCommandRunner|FullyQualifiedName~MxcConfigBuilder"` passed, `63` passed, `4` skipped, `0` failed.
- Final current pass for commit `ddba6f8`: `dotnet test .\tests\OpenClaw.Tray.Tests\OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SettingsRoundTripTests"` passed, `16` passed, `0` failed.
- Final current pass for commit `ddba6f8`: `dotnet build .\src\OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI\OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.csproj --no-restore --configuration Debug --runtime win-x64 --verbosity minimal` passed, `0` warnings, `0` errors.
- Final current pass for commit `ddba6f8`: `openclaw-autoreview --mode local --engine codex --model gpt-5.5 --thinking high` with PR2 contract context ended clean: no accepted/actionable findings, overall `patch is correct`.

Autoreview follow-up on the compatibility-default update found two implementation issues before the final clean run:

- Stale Sandbox InfoBar text for strict fallback enabled plus sandbox toggle off. Fixed by gating strict-blocked unavailable text on the current sandbox toggle state.
- Strict unavailable mode resolving omitted-shell approvals through host fallback. Fixed by keeping `ResolveEffectiveShell(null)` on sandbox `cmd` when `SystemRunBlockHostFallbackWhenMxcUnavailable=true` and MXC is unavailable.

## Runtime Proof Status

Local runtime proof completed on the earlier stacked PR2 checkout for the supported MXC path:

- Direct MXC Gate 1/2 on build `26200.8728`: `wxc-exec --probe` reported `tier=appcontainer-dacl`.
- Positive temp write passed.
- Denied-read smoke did not leak denied content.
- Temp root was removed.
- Isolated Companion MCP-only preflight from patched build output passed using isolated data dirs.
- Temporary narrow MCP `system.run` smoke passed with command `echo OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_RUN_MXC_OK` and returned the expected stdout marker.
- The helper validated log evidence for `[mxc] availability`, `executor=mxc-direct-appc`, `contained=True`, config `version=0.7.0-alpha`, `envKeys=[]`, and `containment=mxc` for the contained supported-path run.

Current `ddba6f8` update is test/build/autoreview-proven for fallback policy, strict unavailable behavior, settings persistence, diagnostics, and `pwsh` launch resolution. I did not rerun the full Gateway-mode runtime proof in this PR2 update; that broader path is intentionally kept for PR3.

## Remaining Publication Limits

- Full Gateway-mode Companion pairing/runtime proof remains unclaimed in PR2.
- Installed Companion replacement/profile migration proof remains unclaimed.
- Broad full-suite green status remains unclaimed.
- The explicit build/UBR table remains a temporary validation strategy; maintainers may still prefer the separate probe-based MXC availability direction.
- PR3 covers broader Gateway-mode validation and installed Companion runtime proof without expanding PR2's scope.

## Notes

This PR does not modify installed Companion state, Gateway pairing, WSL Gateway setup, command allowlists, or permanent `system.run` exposure. The Companion proof used local build output and isolated data directories.

This PR intentionally separates behavior by containment boundary:

- MXC available: use contained processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL execution.
- MXC unavailable with default settings: preserve existing host fallback compatibility.
- MXC unavailable with `SystemRunBlockHostFallbackWhenMxcUnavailable=true`: fail closed.
- Sandbox disabled: explicit host execution.

Broad-suite note: this local split validation does not claim the whole suite is green. Broad runs in this environment hit existing `%APPDATA%`/ACL/`HttpListener` environment failures outside this PR's changed surfaces.
target.author: theangrypit

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
